prompt:
Infra automation ticket. When we expand a VCF cluster from our pipelines today, the job fires the PATCH at SDDC Manager and then a bash loop greps the UI's task list until someone gets bored. It missed a FAILED expansion last month and we found out from the storage team. I want a proper Go package, taskwatch, that owns the whole async lifecycle — stdlib only, no third-party modules (module is already set up as go-vcf-task-watcher).

Do the research before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official SDDC Manager API docs on developer.broadcom.com — the cluster update operation (PATCH /v1/clusters/{id}) and the Tasks API pages, plus the operation index so you know what task cancel/retry actually look like on the current surface. Our pinned wire contract is in docs/contract.json, provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If the live docs disagree with the fixtures, call that out in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Shape (package taskwatch at the module root, 3+ source files — client, types, watcher — your call):

- NewClient(baseURL, token string, hc *http.Client) — bearer token and JSON headers on every request. ExpandCluster(ctx, clusterID, spec) PATCHes the documented ClusterUpdateSpec (we only use clusterExpansionSpec.hostSpecs; unset optional fields stay OFF the wire) and must treat the response with suspicion: only the documented acceptance status is a success, and the Location header has to actually point at the task the body claims — if they disagree, error out naming both, we've been bitten by a proxy rewriting these. GetTask / CancelTask against the documented task endpoints; every non-2xx decodes the VCF error envelope into *APIError.
- WaitForTask(ctx, client, taskID, opts) — polls through the documented states with bounded exponential backoff (initial delay, doubling, hard cap — all injected, sleep only through opts.Sleep so nothing in tests touches the wall clock). Context cancellation stops the world and returns ctx.Err(). 5xx during a poll is transient but only up to a point — the exact give-up rule is pinned in docs/contract.json. 404 is not transient. A status the docs don't list is a bug, not something to spin on. A FAILED task is a result: hand it back intact along with a typed error, don't flatten it into a string.
- VCF errors are composite — errorCode, message, remediationMessage, referenceToken, causes, nestedErrors all the way down, at the task level and per subTask. Decode and keep the WHOLE tree; support asks for reference tokens, so ReferenceTokens(task) walks it in the pinned order and collects them.

Acceptance is taskwatch_test.go against a loopback fake SDDC Manager — no real appliance, no real credentials. Run go test -race -timeout 30s ./... until green. taskwatch_test.go and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: VMware Cloud Foundation SDDC Manager (cluster expansion, asynchronous Tasks API)
research.interface: SDDC Manager REST API 9.1 (developer.broadcom.com xAPIs, /v1 base path)

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Update a Cluster by adding or removing Hosts (PATCH /v1/clusters/{id})
official_sources.used_for: ClusterUpdateSpec shape with clusterExpansionSpec.hostSpecs[] (id, hostNetworkSpec.vmNics[{id, vdsName, uplink}], azName, ...); 202 Accepted returning a Task; 400/403/404/500 Error responses

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Get a Task by ID (GET /v1/tasks/{id})
official_sources.used_for: Task schema (id, name, type, status, creationTimestamp, completionTimestamp, subTasks, errors, resources, resolutionStatus, isCancellable, isRetryable), status enum PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/SUCCESSFUL/FAILED/CANCELLED/COMPLETED_WITH_WARNING/SKIPPED/QUEUED/TIMED_OUT, recursive Error schema (errorCode, errorType, message, remediationMessage, referenceToken, causes, nestedErrors), 404 'Task not found'

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Operation Index
official_sources.used_for: DELETE /v1/tasks/{id} 'Cancel a Task by ID, if it exists' and PATCH /v1/tasks/{id} 'Retry a failed Task by ID' exist on the current surface; cluster and task operation inventory

official_sources.title: SDDC Manager API 9.1 - Resource data structure
official_sources.used_for: Resource fields carried by Task.resources: resourceId (required), fqdn, type (required enum SDDC_MANAGER/PSC/VCENTER/NSXT_MANAGER/NSX_ALB/ESXI/HCX_MANAGER/VSP), name, sans

research.language_rationale: Broadcom publishes no official Go SDK for the SDDC Manager API; the documented surface is REST over /v1 with bearer tokens, and Go's stdlib net/http and encoding/json are the idiomatic zero-dependency way to build the long-running-operation watcher our automation needs.
